headline: Youth Unemployment Rate Doubles National Average at 10.8%
context: DOSM reported 10.8% unemployment among 15-24 year olds in Q4 2025, compared to 5.2% nationally.

cards.big: Youth unemployment at 10.8% is double the national rate of 5.2% in Q4 2025
cards.sub: 388,000 young Malaysians are actively seeking but unable to find work

cards.big: Gig economy absorbs 42% of working youth but with no EPF or SOCSO coverage
cards.sub: Underemployment disguises the true extent of youth economic precarity

cards.big: Minimum wage of RM1,500 has not changed since May 2022 despite 12% cumulative inflation
cards.sub: Real purchasing power of entry-level wages continues to erode

cards.big: ILO reports Malaysia has highest youth NEET rate in ASEAN at 11.3% for 2025
cards.sub: Not in employment education or training is a leading indicator of social exclusion

cards.big: A generation locked out of stable work will not build the families and businesses Malaysia needs
Like building a pipeline that feeds into a reservoir with no outlet — the graduates accumulate, but the jobs to absorb them do not exist at the same rate
cards.sub: Education expansion without proportional job creation creates a bottleneck that hits young workers hardest

cards.big: Should Malaysia introduce a youth employment guarantee program for under-25s
